content.text: The cluster is healthy via cached cert volumes, but the `crdb-cluster-certs` secret is gone. I need to regenerate node certs from the CA and recreate the secret. Let me inspect the existing cert config (SANs) so the new cert matches.

input.command:
echo "=== node.cnf ==="; kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/node.cnf 2>&1
echo "=== existing node.crt SANs/validity ==="; kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- openssl x509 -in /tmp/certs/node.crt -noout -text 2>&1 | grep -A3 -i "subject alternative\|Validity\|Not After\|Not Before\|Subject:"
echo "=== ca validity ==="; kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- openssl x509 -in /tmp/certs/ca.crt -noout -dates 2>&1
input.description: Inspect node cert SANs and config

content.text: The existing node cert is only valid 2 days. I'll regenerate it for 1 year using the existing CSR + CA. First capture the old fingerprint for comparison, then sign a new cert.

input.command:
echo "=== OLD node fingerprint ==="; kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- openssl x509 -in /tmp/certs/node.crt -noout -fingerprint -sha256 2>&1
echo "=== regenerate node cert (365 days) ==="
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c '
cd /tmp/certs && \
openssl x509 -req -in node.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -out node.crt -days 365 -sha256 -extfile node.cnf -extensions v3_req 2>&1 && \
echo "--- new validity ---" && openssl x509 -in node.crt -noout -dates && \
echo "--- new SANs ---" && openssl x509 -in node.crt -noout -ext subjectAltName && \
echo "--- new fingerprint ---" && openssl x509 -in node.crt -noout -fingerprint -sha256
'

content.text: New node cert is valid 1 year with correct SANs. Now regenerate the client.root cert too (it likely also expires in 2 days) so SQL keeps working long-term.

input.command:
echo "=== old client.root dates ==="; kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- openssl x509 -in /tmp/certs/client.root.crt -noout -dates -subject 2>&1
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c '
cd /tmp/certs && \
printf "[v3_client]\nkeyUsage = critical,digitalSignature,keyEncipherment\nextendedKeyUsage = clientAuth\nsubjectAltName = DNS:root\n" > client.cnf && \
openssl x509 -req -in client.root.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -out client.root.crt -days 365 -sha256 -extfile client.cnf -extensions v3_client 2>&1 && \
echo "--- new client dates ---" && openssl x509 -in client.root.crt -noout -dates -subject -ext subjectAltName
'

content.text: Both certs rotated for 1 year. Now I'll recreate the `crdb-cluster-certs` secret from the toolbox files. Let me pull the cert contents and build the secret.

input.command:
cd /tmp && mkdir -p crdb-certs && cd crdb-certs
for f in ca.crt node.crt node.key client.root.crt client.root.key; do
  kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/$f > $f 2>/dev/null
done
ls -la
echo "=== sizes sanity ==="; wc -c *

input.command:
cd /tmp/crdb-certs
kubectl -n cockroachdb create secret generic crdb-cluster-certs \
  --from-file=ca.crt=ca.crt \
  --from-file=node.crt=node.crt \
  --from-file=node.key=node.key \
  --from-file=client.root.crt=client.root.crt \
  --from-file=client.root.key=client.root.key 2>&1
echo "=== verify secret ==="
kubectl -n cockroachdb get secret crdb-cluster-certs -o jsonpath='{.data}' 2>&1 | tr ',' '\n' | sed 's/:.*//'

content.text: Secret recreated with all 5 keys. Now I'll do a rolling restart of the statefulset so pods pick up the new certs, and wait for it to complete.

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb rollout restart statefulset crdb-cluster 2>&1
echo "=== waiting for rollout ==="
kubectl -n cockroachdb rollout status statefulset crdb-cluster --timeout=300s 2>&1
